response: solution The primary error in the erroneous code lies in its failure to handle duplicate elements effectively, often resulting in adjacent equal values when the array contains many repeated numbers. solution A robust approach to fix this is to first sort the array and then interleave elements by placing the smallest half in the odd indices and the largest half in the even indices, starting from the end of the sorted halves to avoid collisions. solution For the follow-up requirement of O(n) time complexity without sorting, one can use the QuickSelect algorithm to find the median, partition the array around it, and then apply the same interleaving strategy. solution This method ensures that even with negative integers, the relative ordering and the wiggle property `nums[i] <= nums[i+1] >= nums[i+2]` are maintained correctly. solution The final implementation must verify that if equal adjacent elements are unavoidable due to frequency constraints, the array is returned as is, though the problem guarantees a valid answer exists.
